agent_building_workflow.overview: This workflow guides you through building a complete watsonx Orchestrate agent from requirements gathering to deployment. You will interview the user, design the agent architecture, generate all necessary files, and deploy the agent end-to-end. This workflow is domain-agnostic and can be applied to any industry or use case.

core_principles.principle: Interview first, build second — gather complete requirements before generating code
Use the portfolio-advisor-agent as the foundation for all new agents

⚠️ CRITICAL: During deployment, ask user for API key and activate env via piping: echo "KEY" | uvx --from ibm-watsonx-orchestrate orchestrate env activate wxo-uv-env — do NOT put env activate inside scripts
⚠️ CRITICAL: Use EXACT CLI commands — `tools` not `tool`, `agents` not `agent`, `knowledge-bases` not `kb`, `-f` not `--path`
⚠️ CRITICAL: Always use llm: groq/openai/gpt-oss-120b — DO NOT use other LLMs
⚠️ CRITICAL: Limit agents to maximum 5 tools for optimal performance

phase.name: requirements_gathering
phase.title: Requirements Gathering Interview
phase.description: Conduct a structured interview to understand the domain and gather all necessary information for building the agent. This is the most critical phase - get complete information upfront.

question_group.question: What is the domain or industry for this agent? (Examples: Healthcare, Retail, Finance, Education, Manufacturing, Hospitality, Real Estate, Insurance, Logistics, Legal Services, etc.)
What is the primary purpose of this agent? What problems will it solve?
Who are the primary users or entities this agent will serve? (Examples: customers, patients, students, employees, members, clients, partners, etc.)
What is the business context or use case for this agent?
What do you call the main entities in your domain? (Examples: "customers", "patients", "students", "members", "clients", "users", "subscribers", "partners", "vendors", "employees")

Do you have tiers, segments, levels, or categories for these entities? If yes, what are they called and what do they represent? (Examples: Basic/Premium/VIP, Bronze/Silver/Gold, Standard/Plus/Pro, Free/Paid/Enterprise, Tier 1/2/3/4)
What key information do you track about each entity? (Examples: contact info, preferences, history, metrics, status, relationships)
Are there any unique identifiers or reference numbers used in your domain?
What are the 3-5 main capabilities this agent should have? (Examples: "Retrieve entity data", "Generate personalized messages", "Calculate metrics", "Process requests", "Provide recommendations")
What types of communications or messages should the agent generate? (Examples: notifications, alerts, reports, recommendations, confirmations, reminders, updates, summaries)
What actions or operations should the agent be able to perform? (Examples: search, filter, calculate, schedule, update, create, delete)
Are there any specific calculations, analytics, or business rules the agent should implement?
Should the agent integrate with any external systems or APIs?
What data sources will the agent use? (Examples: CSV files, databases, APIs, CRM systems, spreadsheets, documents)
Do you have sample data available, or should I generate realistic sample data?
Are there multiple data files or tables to integrate? (Examples: entity data, location data, transaction data, product data, reference data)
What is the typical structure of your data? What fields are most important?
What should we name this agent? (Provide a short, descriptive name like "customer_service_agent" or "inventory_manager")
Do you have your WXO API key ready? Note: You will be asked for this key during deployment.
Where should the agent project be created? (Default: ./[agent-name]-agent, parallel to .bob/)
Are there any specific branding or styling preferences for communications?

interview_strategy.step: Start with domain basics to understand the context
Ask follow-up questions based on user's answers
Probe for domain-specific terminology and concepts
Ask if there are any special requirements or constraints
Identify any compliance or regulatory considerations

completion_criteria.criterion: Entity type and tier/segment structure identified

phase.name: architecture_design
phase.title: Agent Architecture Design
phase.description: Based on gathered requirements, design the agent architecture including entity model, tool structure, and data schema.

design_tasks.task: Map domain requirements to agent configuration structure
Design entity data model with appropriate fields
Plan tool functions based on required capabilities
Design communication templates for each message type
Plan knowledge base structure and data files

pattern.name: entity_management
pattern.description: Core tools for retrieving and managing entities (3-4 tools recommended)
pattern.customization: Adapt function names and parameters to domain terminology

pattern.name: communication_generation
pattern.description: Tools for generating personalized communications (1 tool recommended)

functions.function: _get_template() - Template selection helper (internal, not a tool)

pattern.customization: Define message types relevant to the domain

pattern.name: domain_specific_operations
pattern.description: Tools for domain-specific business operations (0-1 tool recommended)
pattern.customization: Create tools that match domain workflows

components.component: Primary entity data (CSV or structured format)

design_considerations.consideration: Ensure tier/segment structure matches business model
Consider data privacy and security requirements
Plan for realistic sample data that represents domain diversity

phase.name: project_setup
phase.title: Project Directory Setup
phase.description: Create the project directory by copying the portfolio-advisor-agent template.

step.action: Copy portfolio-advisor-agent to new project directory

step.expected: Should see tools/, data/, knowledge_bases/, scripts/, etc.

phase.name: file_generation
phase.title: Generate All Agent Files
phase.description: Generate or customize all required files based on requirements and design. Replace all placeholders with domain-specific values.

file.description: Main agent configuration — replace all placeholders (name, display_name, description, domain, entity types, tiers, capabilities, communication types)
Deployment scripts — update agent name references and test queries
file.note: Scripts must NOT call env activate — Bob handles env activation by piping API key during Phase 5
Use EXACT commands: tools import (not tool), agents import (not agent), knowledge-bases import (not kb)
Use -f for file, -k for kind, -n for name

file.description: Entity management tools — copy from account_holder_tools.py, rename, update fields, functions, and embedded sample data
Communication generation tools — customize message types, HTML templates, personalization logic, and tier-specific styling
Additional domain-specific tools — create only if domain requires specialized operations
Main entity data — define domain-appropriate columns, generate 5-10 realistic sample records with diverse tier representation
Additional reference data (locations, categories, etc.) — 3-5 sample records
Domain-specific documentation, policies, procedures, or FAQs
Knowledge base configuration — update name, description, document paths, and metadata
Project documentation — agent overview, domain context, quick start, examples, deployment steps, troubleshooting

phase.name: deployment
phase.title: Deploy Agent to WXO
phase.description: Deploy the complete agent to watsonx Orchestrate.

step.action: Ask user for their WXO API key and activate environment
step.command: echo "API_KEY" | uvx --from ibm-watsonx-orchestrate orchestrate env activate wxo-uv-env
step.note: Ask the user for their API key, then pipe it to env activate. This avoids the interactive prompt that hangs in non-interactive contexts. Run this BEFORE deploy_all.sh.

step.action: Monitor deployment output and verify each step completes successfully
Confirm agent is live in WXO interface

issue.name: api_key_missing
issue.symptom: Error about missing API key or authentication
issue.solution: Ask user for API key and pipe to env activate

issue.name: kb_import_fails
issue.solution: Verify YAML syntax and data file paths

issue.name: agent_deploy_fails
issue.solution: Check agent_config.yaml syntax and tool references

phase.name: testing_and_validation
phase.title: Test Agent in WXO
phase.description: After deployment, test the agent with sample queries to verify all tools work correctly.

testing_approach.principle: Start with simple queries, progress to complex ones
Verify data access works (especially for embedded data)
Test with sample IDs from embedded data

category.name: entity_retrieval
category.description: Test basic entity lookup and filtering

sample_queries.query: Filter by tier (e.g., "Show me Premium customers")

common_issues.issue: Data not found — indicates embedded data not loaded
File access errors — indicates CSV loading instead of embedded data
Empty results — indicates filter logic issues

sample_queries.query: Get statistics by tier (e.g., "Get stats for Premium tier")
Generate report for specific entity (e.g., "Generate progress report for STU001")
Test different message types (e.g., "Create achievement notification")

common_issues.issue: "Entity not found" — indicates data access problem (CRITICAL)
Generic output — indicates personalization not working
Missing data fields — indicates incomplete embedded data

step.action: Access agent in WXO interface — verify it appears and is accessible
Test entity retrieval with known IDs from embedded data
step.if_fails: Check if tool uses embedded data vs file loading; fix and re-import

step.action: Test communication generation with known entity ID
step.if_fails: Most common: "Entity not found" — tool loading from CSV instead of embedded data

step.action: Test error handling with non-existent entity ID
Document test results — which queries worked, which failed, issues discovered

common_issue.name: entity_not_found
common_issue.symptom: "Entity with ID XXX not found" error
common_issue.root_cause: Tool loading data from CSV files instead of embedded data
common_issue.solution: Embed data as Python dicts, remove CSV loading code, re-import tools, redeploy agent

common_issue.name: tool_not_responding
common_issue.symptom: Tool doesn't execute or times out
common_issue.solution: Check tool logs, review code for errors, fix and re-import

common_issue.name: incorrect_output
common_issue.symptom: Tool returns wrong or incomplete data
common_issue.solution: Verify embedded data structure, check field names match, review logic

success_criteria.criterion: All entity retrieval queries return correct data
Communication generation works with embedded data
Metrics and calculations produce accurate results
No "file not found" or "entity not found" errors

when_to_proceed.condition: All critical test queries pass successfully
Any issues discovered have been fixed and re-tested
when_to_proceed.note: Do NOT proceed to documentation if critical tests fail

phase.name: documentation_and_handoff
phase.title: Documentation and Handoff
phase.description: Provide comprehensive documentation and next steps. Only proceed after successful testing.

deliverables.deliverable: BUSINESS_USE_CASE.md — business use case summary with sample queries

step.description: Create a markdown file in the agent project root that explains the business use case and provides ready-to-use sample queries as a quick-start guide.

section.name: Business Use Case
section.content: 1-2 paragraph description of the business problem, target users, and value

section.name: Agent Capabilities
section.content: Bulleted list grouped by capability (entity lookup, filtering, communication, analytics)

section.name: Sample Queries
section.content: 8-12 ready-to-use queries organized by category: entity retrieval, filtered queries, metrics/analytics, communication generation, open-ended KB questions. Use real entity IDs from embedded data.

section.name: Example Interaction
section.content: One representative query with expected response

section.name: Entity Model
section.content: Brief description of tiers/segments and what each represents

step.description: Summarize what was built and point user to BUSINESS_USE_CASE.md for sample queries

section.name: what_was_built
section.item: Agent name, purpose, domain, and use case
Key capabilities, tools, and entity model

section.name: how_to_test
section.item: Refer to BUSINESS_USE_CASE.md for ready-to-use sample queries
How to access agent in WXO

section.name: next_steps
section.item: Replace sample data with real data
Integrate with external APIs or systems
Set up monitoring and gather feedback

critical_reminders.reminder: Always interview BEFORE building — gather complete requirements first
Use portfolio-advisor-agent as the foundation — it's proven and deployed
Generate COMPLETE files with real values — no placeholders or TODOs in final code
⚠️ CRITICAL: During deployment, ask user for API key and PIPE to env activate — do NOT put env activate in scripts or use .env files
⚠️ CRITICAL: CLI commands must be EXACT — `tools` (plural), `agents` (plural), `knowledge-bases` (not kb), `-f` (not --path)
⚠️ CRITICAL: Limit agents to MAXIMUM 5 tools for optimal performance

rule.incorrect: NEVER use kind: internal or embedding_model:
